_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — within-tier from Knight Sergeant (L7) to Knight (L8, Tier 3 mid). Apply ADDITIVE upgrades — kettle helm preserved with new half-visor extending downward from the brim and a gold trim band at the brim seam; pauldrons grow to 3-lame; tassets extend longer with 2 wider lames per tasset; full sabatons complete the boot-armor split. NO horse-barding add at L8 (crinet moved to L9 per AD detail-economy).
_meta.input_image: image_urls[0] = UnitProgression/Cavalry/out/v1/L7/composite/cavalry_L7_v1_composited.png ; image_urls[1] = UnitProgression/Cavalry/Refs/L1_Base.png (scale anchor) — placeholder keeper version, user will rewire to actual keeper at runtime.
history: v1 (2026-05-07): initial author — Knight (Tier 3 mid) within-tier with 5 ADDITIVE adds per CLAUDE.md § Part 2 § K0 + AD FeedbackV0 detail-economy. (1) Half-visor extending DOWNWARD from the kettle brim covering forehead-to-nose-bridge with horizontal eye slit (kettle base preserved). (2) FIRST GOLD = gold trim band wrapping the kettle helm at the brim line (replaces the deprecated 'gold rivets on pauldrons' beat — Infantry's lesson that gold rivets soft-fail at thumbnail; a clear gold band on the helm body lands the first gold cleanly). (3) 3-lame pauldrons (third lame extends down the bicep). (4) Tassets extend longer with 2 wider lames per tasset (NOT 3 narrow lames per AD detail-economy). (5) Full sabatons on boots — final boot-armor stage in the L4→L6→L8 split per AD 'boot armor split into smaller upgrades'. NO horse-barding add at L8 (crinet moved to L9). Locked composite thresholds 15/35 pool=12 (no tier-break dilate at L8 — within-tier).
v3 (2026-05-07): pipeline update — drop L1_Base scale anchor from image_urls and prompt body. Single-image input (prior-level composited keeper only) per updated pipeline + docs.
v4 (2026-05-07): scrub belt-studs PRESERVE/negative references — L2 IRON STUDS beat dropped per AD detail-economy.
v6 (2026-05-07): added prophylactic anti-rivet/stud guardrail negative — same as Infantry chain, applied through the Cavalry chain after rivet bleed-through observed at L3 generation.

v7 (2026-05-07): reframe L8-3 pauldron add to single-piece coverage extension (was 3-stacked-lames + decorative rivet row). Aligns with L6 single-piece thicker dome reframe; no stacked-plate construction across the L4-L8-L10 ramp.
v7 (2026-05-07): scrub stale "2-disc layered pauldrons" PRESERVE refs — L6 was reframed to single-piece thicker dome (no stacked-disc construction).

v8 (2026-05-07): retone all metal-color language from cool silver-gray to warm silver-gray matching the rendered L1 kettle-helm tone — cool/blueish steel was reading distinctly different from the L1 baseline's warm gray, getting more pronounced at higher levels. Added negative clause locking out cool/blue-gray steel palette.

v9 (2026-05-07): scrub stale chest-band PRESERVE/negative references — L6 chest-band-above-breastplate was removed (user feedback: cloth-above-armor looks impractical). Cuirass now sits flush against the bevor; only the skirt band of livery remains on the rider.
v10 (2026-05-07): retone metal language from 'warm gray' -> 'neutral gray' (prior pass overshot warm; balanced gray with neither blue nor brown shadow tint matches the L1 baseline most accurately).

v11 (2026-05-07): scrub model-invisible context from prompt body — removed L<n> level-tag references (model is stateless and cannot resolve our level naming), authoring-decision parentheticals (LOCKED/REVISED/REMOVED dates, per AD FeedbackV0, etc.), and cross-level range refs. All persistent decisions remain in _meta.history + the progression plan + CLAUDE.md. See Archer/CLAUDE.md § Part 3 locked decision #27 (drift-corrected: residual parenthetical level-tags also dropped).

adds.title: HALF-VISOR EXTENDING DOWNWARD FROM THE KETTLE HELM'S FRONT BRIM
adds.summary: KEEP the L1-L7 kettle helm exactly with its L7 backswept neck-guard skirt and brim reinforcement and additional brim rivets.

adds.title: GOLD TRIM BAND WRAPPING THE KETTLE HELM AT THE BRIM LINE
adds.summary: add a buttery polished yellow gold metal band wrapping the full circumference of the kettle helm at the brim line, sitting at the seam between the kettle's domed crown and its forward-projecting brim.

adds.title: PAULDRONS GAIN BICEP COVERAGE EXTENSION (SINGLE-PIECE)
adds.summary: extend each L6 thicker single-piece pauldron dome DOWNWARD to cover the upper bicep — single continuous piece of shaped steel covering shoulder + upper bicep (NOT a stacked third lame, NOT a separate disc layered on top). Coverage extension carries the L8 upgrade per AD detail-economy.

adds.title: TASSETS EXTEND LONGER
adds.summary: 2 WIDER LAMES PER TASSET (NOT 3 narrow lames) — the L6 short hip tassets (2 wider lames each) GROW DOWNWARD to cover mid-thigh.

adds.title: FULL STEEL SABATONS ON THE BOOTS
adds.summary: completing the boot-armor split (L4 toe-cap → L6 ankle plate → L8 full sabaton) per AD FeedbackV0 'boot armor split into smaller upgrades, distributed earlier'.

image_urls: <<UPLOAD: UnitProgression/Cavalry/out/v1/L7/composite/cavalry_L7_v1_composited.png>> (content reference — apply the upgrades onto this character)
prompt: EDIT GOAL — Apply FIVE visible ADDITIVE upgrades to the mounted cavalry knight in the FIRST input image, marking the within-tier promotion from Knight Sergeant to Knight. Every the levels above detail must remain visible at this level. The rider already wears the existing Knight Sergeant kit: kettle helm with backswept neck-guard skirt and brim reinforcement and additional brim rivets (kettle silhouette stays at this level — the new half-visor extends downward from the kettle's brim, the kettle base is preserved); mustache (no beard, clean-shaven cheeks/chin); steel bevor at the throat; full steel cuirass with medial ridge and 1 simple horizontal abdominal seam, with skirt band of yellow+red livery only (cuirass flush against bevor, no chest band); full-arm scale-mail sleeves bicep-to-wrist (chunky overlapping rounded scale plates); thicker single-piece steel pauldron domes on both shoulders (the new third lower lame is added at per Edit Goal item 3); steel vambraces on both forearms layered on top of the scale mail; steel finger-plates + clean knuckle disc layered on top of the existing bare-hand silhouettes (with the existing leather grip cord visible between the gauntleted fingers and the wooden haft); leather waist belt with plain leather belt and brass buckle; short steel tassets at both hips with 2 wider lames each (the tassets EXTEND longer at per Edit Goal item 4); iron toe-cap strip on each boot AND ankle plate on each boot (full sabatons complete at per Edit Goal item 5); blue trousers; brown leather riding boots in stirrups; wooden spear haft (warm-brown wood, with iron pommel cap, leather grip cord, iron banding rings + reinforced socket, steel leaf-tip head + red+yellow heraldic pennon); hip pouch on spear-side hip (the only belt accessory). The horse already wears: full steel chamfron with small forehead spike, iron-buckled bridle with iron browband, iron banding on saddle pommel and cantle, iron peytral plate beneath the red+blue checkered caparison. THE HORSE IS SOLID CHESTNUT BROWN ACROSS THE ENTIRE BODY — no white markings of any kind. CRITICAL SPATIAL ANCHOR — treat 'spear-side' as the side of the rider's body / canvas where the upright wooden spear haft is visible (the side closer to the horse's tail), and 'rein-side' as the OPPOSITE side where the rein hand grips the reins. Throughout this prompt, do NOT use 'right' or 'left' to interpret placement; use SPEAR-SIDE / REIN-SIDE only. (1) HALF-VISOR EXTENDING DOWNWARD FROM THE KETTLE HELM'S FRONT BRIM (additive — NOT replacing the kettle) — KEEP the the levels above kettle helm exactly with its backswept neck-guard skirt and brim reinforcement and additional brim rivets. ADD a steel half-visor that extends DOWNWARD from the FORWARD brim of the kettle, covering the upper half of the face from forehead down to nose-bridge level. The half-visor leaves the EYES VISIBLE through a horizontal eye slit ~0.5 inch tall running across the visor at eye height. The kettle's domed CROWN remains visible above the new visor; the visor's upper edge attaches at the kettle's brim seam. Cool silver-gray steel matching the kettle body. The mustache, mouth, jaw, and chin remain visible BELOW the half-visor's lower edge. (2) GOLD TRIM BAND WRAPPING THE KETTLE HELM AT THE BRIM LINE (FIRST GOLD on the unit) — add a buttery polished yellow gold metal band wrapping the full circumference of the kettle helm at the brim line, sitting at the seam between the kettle's domed crown and its forward-projecting brim. The gold band is approximately 1 inch tall, visible all the way around the kettle from front to back. RENDER THE GOLD AS BUTTERY POLISHED YELLOW GOLD METAL — saturated warm-yellow hue contrasting strongly against the neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) of the kettle, distinctly more yellow than the surrounding steel — NOT brass, NOT bronze, NOT yellow paint, NOT a mustard tint, NOT a warm-tinted highlight on steel. Render with: (i) saturated buttery-yellow base, (ii) soft top-down highlight that reads almost white-yellow, (iii) deeper warm shadow on the underside of the band that reads honey-orange. The gold band must be IMMEDIATELY READABLE as gold at thumbnail scale. This is the FIRST gold accent on the unit — no gold appears anywhere else on or earlier. (3) PAULDRONS GAIN BICEP COVERAGE EXTENSION (SINGLE-PIECE) — extend each thicker single-piece pauldron dome DOWNWARD to cover the upper-bicep portion of the arm. The pauldron now reads as ONE LARGER PIECE from above the shoulder cap down to mid-bicep — the existing thicker dome at the top, transitioning at a single clean curved steel-on-steel seam into a smooth lower section that wraps the upper third of the bicep below the dome. NOT a stacked-plate construction; NOT a separate third disc layered onto the existing dome; NOT a hinged lame riveted to the dome — the existing pauldron reads as ONE CONTINUOUS PIECE OF SHAPED STEEL covering shoulder + upper bicep. Both pauldrons get the bicep extension symmetrically. The pauldrons visibly extend the shoulder silhouette wider and downward (silhouette mightiness rule — coverage extension carries the existing upgrade). The new pauldron extension sits ON TOP of the full-arm scale-mail sleeve at the bicep; the scale mail is visible only where the pauldron extension does not cover it (between the pauldron's lower edge and the steel vambrace at the forearm). NO decorative rivet rows along any pauldron seam. NO sculpted ornament on the dome or the bicep extension. (4) TASSETS EXTEND LONGER — 2 WIDER LAMES PER TASSET (NOT 3 narrow lames) — the existing short hip tassets (2 wider lames each) GROW DOWNWARD to cover mid-thigh. Each tasset still has EXACTLY 2 wider lames stacked vertically (NOT 3 narrow lames — wider plates, fewer of them,). The upper lame is now ~4 inches tall and ~7 inches wide, the lower lame is ~5 inches tall and ~8 inches wide with a more pronounced outward flare at the bottom edge. Joined by a single row of small dark-iron rivets along the seam where the lower lame overlaps the upper lame. The skirt band of yellow+red livery between the cuirass's bottom edge and the new tassets' top edge is now narrower (~2 inches tall, both panels still distinguishable). (5) FULL STEEL SABATONS ON THE BOOTS — completing the boot-armor split (toe-cap → ankle plate → full sabaton) 'boot armor split into smaller upgrades, distributed earlier'. The iron toe-cap strip on the front of each toe and the existing steel ankle plate around each ankle are now JOINED by continuous neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) covering the front of the foot (toe top + foot top + instep + ankle continuous metal plate). The brown leather body of each boot remains visible at the calf above the sabaton and at the heel. Both boots have matching full sabatons. The full-foot continuous steel surface IS the new add — the existing toe-cap and ankle plate are now visible as integrated portions of the continuous sabaton silhouette rather than as separate pieces. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the FIRST input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion. THE RIDER FACE: chiseled chibi jawline; thick dark-brown eyebrows; large warm steel-blue eyes visible through the new half-visor's eye slit; prominent slightly-hooked nose visible just below the half-visor's lower edge; warm peachy-tan skin tone; THICK DARK-BROWN HANDLEBAR MUSTACHE handlebar-shaped covering the upper lip — visible below the half-visor; cheeks and chin CLEAN-SHAVEN with NO beard, NO stubble. THE RIDER HEAD COVERING: the the levels above kettle helm with its backswept neck-guard skirt, brim reinforcement, additional brim rivets — unchanged at this level. The new half-visor extends downward from the front brim per Edit Goal item (1). The new gold trim band wraps the brim line per Edit Goal item (2). NO sallet, NO closed visor at this level (closed visor arrives at this level). THE RIDER NECK: the existing steel bevor / collar plate at the throat, unchanged. THE RIDER TORSO: the existing reinforced cuirass with medial ridge + 1 horizontal abdominal seam — unchanged; the skirt band of yellow+red livery — still visible (narrower at due to longer tassets); the cuirass sits flush against the bevor (NO chest band of cloth above the cuirass); the wide brown leather waist belt with brass buckle (plain leather, no studs). THE RIDER ARMS AND HANDS: the EXISTING FULL-ARM scale-mail sleeves; the EXISTING steel vambraces on the forearms layered on top of the scale mail (unchanged); the EXISTING steel finger-plates + clean knuckle disc layered on top of the bare-hand silhouettes; both hands gripping the wooden spear haft (spear-side) and the brown leather reins (rein-side). THE RIDER LEGS: dark blue / muted steel-blue knee-length trousers visible at the calf below the new full sabatons; chunky warm-brown leather riding boots — the body of each boot still visible at the calf above the sabaton and at the heel; both boots in stirrups. THE WEAPON: the wooden spear haft warm-brown wood (the levels above unchanged) with the existing iron pommel cap, leather grip cord, iron banding rings + reinforced socket, steel leaf-tip head + red+yellow heraldic pennon — all unchanged at this level. THE REINS: the brown leather reins at the horse's withers, gripped relaxed by the gauntleted rein-side hand. THE HORSE: the SOLID chestnut-brown chibi horse facing the viewer's RIGHT — head and muzzle on the rein-side, rump and tail on the spear-side, four-square stance, neck arched, ears forward. THE HORSE IS SOLID CHESTNUT BROWN ACROSS THE ENTIRE BODY — NO WHITE MARKINGS — no white blaze, no white star, no white socks, no white speckling. Mane and tail dark brown. THE HORSE TACK: the brown leather saddle with iron banding on pommel and cantle; the iron-buckled bridle with iron browband; the existing full steel chamfron with forehead spike — UNCHANGED at this level (no horse-barding add at this level; crinet arrives at this level); the iron peytral plate beneath the caparison. THE CAPARISON: the large rectangular cloth caparison with red+blue checkered pattern and steel-blue trim band along the lower edge with shallow rectangular tabs — UNCHANGED at this level. THE EXISTING the levels above KIT: ALL preserved unchanged in shape, color, and position (other than the existing short tassets growing longer per Edit Goal item 4 and the the existing separate boot-armor pieces becoming continuous sabatons per Edit Goal item 5). NO knife sheath at any hip (removed at). The hip pouch remains as the only belt accessory. ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, the rider's body angled approximately 15-25 degrees toward the viewer's left. The HORSE faces toward the viewer's RIGHT. Rider sits upright in the saddle with both feet in stirrups, slight forward lean. THE SPEAR ARM (the rider's arm on the spear-side of the frame) is bent at the elbow with the upper arm vertical and the forearm angled slightly forward; the gauntleted hand grips the wooden spear haft at the existing leather grip cord wrap. THE REIN ARM (the rider's arm on the rein-side of the frame) is extended forward and slightly down, elbow bent at roughly 110 degrees; the gauntleted hand grips the brown leather reins at the level of the horse's withers / shoulder. The pose reads as a calm mounted patrol stance, with a clearly mightier silhouette than (broader pauldrons via 3rd lame, longer tassets, full sabatons, half-visor adding downward face coverage). NOT a charge, NOT a couched-lance attack, NOT a rear, NOT a pivot. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal and leather. Clean thin black silhouette outline around the entire rider+horse+spear+caparison silhouette in deep warm brown-black. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match these canonical numbers exactly — exactly, with small vertical extent acceptable from the existing silhouette growth) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Rider+horse silhouette occupies approximately 77.6% of canvas vertical height (small growth acceptable from the longer tassets and broader pauldrons). The wooden spear haft extends UP past the helm with the existing steel leaf-tip near or at the canvas top edge. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT crop the helm or hooves. Clean opaque WHITE background — no light gray tint, no off-white shading. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT replace the kettle helm with a sallet, closed helm, conical helm, or any other helm shape — the kettle STAYS at this level (the half-visor extends downward from the kettle's brim, the kettle's domed crown remains visible above the new visor); DO NOT add a closed visor or full visor at this level — the half-visor leaves the eyes visible through a horizontal eye slit (closed visor arrives at this level); DO NOT add helm horns, helm crest, plumes, or fins to the kettle at this level (fins arrive at this level); DO NOT add gold rivets on pauldrons, gold pauldron-seam decorations, gold filigree, gold chased patterns, or gold piping anywhere — the FIRST gold at is the kettle helm trim band ONLY (the deprecated 'gold rivets on pauldrons' beat is REMOVED + Infantry's lesson that pauldron-seam gold soft-fails at thumbnail); DO NOT make the gold band on the kettle small, faint, or low-contrast — it must read as buttery polished yellow gold immediately at thumbnail scale; DO NOT add gold trim anywhere else on the rider or horse at this level (no gold pauldron piping, no gold cuirass edge, no gold tasset trim, no gold sabaton inlay, no gold belt accents, no gold lance head); DO NOT break the pauldrons into 4 or more lames at this level — exactly 3 lames per pauldron (small upper disc + larger lower disc + new third lame extending down the bicep); DO NOT break the tassets into 3 or more narrow lames at this level — exactly 2 wider lames per tasset; DO NOT shrink the tassets back to length — they EXTEND downward to cover mid-thigh at this level; DO NOT erase or reduce the skirt band of yellow+red livery — it remains visible (may narrow but both yellow and red panels still distinguishable); DO NOT re-add a chest band of cloth above the cuirass — the cuirass sits flush against the bevor; DO NOT add a crinet (neck plates — arrives at this level), flanchards (arrive at this level), or any other horse-barding add at this level; DO NOT change the horse coat color from solid chestnut brown; DO NOT add any white markings on the horse — no white blaze, no white star, no white socks, no white speckling; DO NOT remove the existing full chamfron with forehead spike on the horse; DO NOT change the caparison's red+blue checkered pattern, steel-blue trim band, or tabbed lower edge; DO NOT add any heraldic emblem, cross, lion, eagle, or coat-of-arms to the caparison or surcoat at this level; DO NOT replace the wooden spear haft with a steel haft — the wooden haft STAYS warm-brown wood with all its accumulated kit; DO NOT erase the existing leather grip cord, iron banding rings, reinforced socket, steel leaf-tip head, or red+yellow heraldic pennon; DO NOT make the pennon any color other than red+yellow split heraldic; DO NOT add a knife sheath, dagger sheath, dagger, or any second belt accessory; DO NOT make the new full sabatons gold or brass — they are neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) only; DO NOT cover the brown leather body of the boots at the calf or heel — the leather body remains visible above the sabaton and at the heel; DO NOT change the horse's pose; DO NOT make the horse rear, walk, gallop, pivot, or move; DO NOT change the horse to a pony, hobby-horse, wooden cutout horse, donkey, mule, or any other species; DO NOT add iron rivets, studs, embossed dots, or any small metal punctuation along the leather waist belt, the leather under-jacket sleeves, the surcoat hem, the saddle leather, the bridle straps, the caparison panels, the rider's hands, or any surface beyond what the input image already shows; DO NOT add metal banding rings or riveted hardware appearing as decoration on the kettle-helm body, the surcoat, or the wooden spear haft middle (the existing kettle's existing brim rivets are functional construction rivets and stay unchanged); DO NOT change the chibi proportions; DO NOT change the camera angle or composition; DO NOT crop the helm or hooves; DO NOT change the background, add scenery, or add a ground plane; DO NOT make the background transparent, checkerboard-patterned, light-gray, off-white, or any non-white color; DO NOT drift the art style; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, blue rim light, cyan rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square; DO NOT grow the mustache into a beard or add stubble or a five o'clock shadow.
